100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 29, 1926, tensions escalated in the Middle East as reports emerged of increasing unrest in Palestine, highlighting growing friction between Jewish settlers and Arab residents. Meanwhile, in Europe, the League of Nations faced criticism for its inability to effectively address the ongoing economic struggles after World War I. In the United States, the film industry buzzed with excitement as the premiere of "The Son of the Sheik" showcased Rudolph Valentino’s return to the silver screen, promising to captivate audiences once more.
Updated: Monday, June 29, 2026 at 10:21:16 AM CDT
